case_metadata.court: OHIO FIRST DISTRICT COURT OF APPEALS

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: In 2013, Ms. Yeakle went to the emergency room after a fall related to a problem with her right knee.
evidence_index.summary: Ms. Yeakle visited the ER in 2013 due to a knee issue.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Dr. Durrani was called to consult on whether the problem could be neurological.
evidence_index.paragraph_starts_with: problem with her right knee. At
evidence_index.summary: Dr. Durrani consulted on potential neurological issues.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Dr. Durrani scheduled surgery to correct these issues, but on the day of surgery, Dr. Durrani was unavailable.
evidence_index.summary: Dr. Durrani scheduled surgery but was unavailable on the day.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Dr. Shanti, another surgeon in Dr. Durrani’s practice, filled in for Dr. Durrani and performed the surgery.
evidence_index.paragraph_starts_with: issues, but on the day of surgery,
evidence_index.summary: Dr. Shanti performed the surgery instead of Dr. Durrani.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Following the surgery, Ms. Yeakle suffered significant back pain that had not existed previously.
evidence_index.summary: Post-surgery, Ms. Yeakle experienced new back pain.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: The jury found Dr. Durrani liable for medical negligence and lack of informed consent.
evidence_index.summary: Jury found Dr. Durrani liable for negligence and lack of consent.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: The trial court’s judgments denying appellants’ motion for a new trial and appellants’ motion for a directed verdict on the claim for lack of informed consent are reversed.
evidence_index.summary: Court reversed denial of new trial and directed verdict motions.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Construing the evidence most strongly in favor of Ms. Yeakle, reasonable minds could conclude that Dr. Durrani was responsible for medical negligence in his diagnosis and subsequent recommendation for a surgery.
evidence_index.summary: Court found evidence could support negligence by Dr. Durrani.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: The record contained competing expert testimony as to the necessity of the surgery and informed consent.
evidence_index.summary: Experts disagreed on surgery necessity and informed consent.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

verdict_detected.key_phrase: The trial court’s judgments denying appellants’ motion for a new trial and appellants’ motion for a directed verdict on the claim for lack of informed consent are reversed.

initial_state.chief_complaint: Right knee problem after a fall
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: In 2013, Ms. Yeakle went to the emergency room after a fall related to a problem with her right knee.

focused_exam.knee: Examination findings not specified in judgment

initial_state.initial_working_problem: Right knee problem - potential neurological involvement

requestables.name: Neurological Consultation
requestables.description: Consultation by Dr. Durrani to assess neurological involvement

reveal.clinical_significance: Surgery deemed necessary by Dr. Durrani

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient with knee problem potentially involving neurological issues
decision_points.prompt: Should surgery be scheduled based on the consultation?

options.clinical_reasoning: Surgery was scheduled by Dr. Durrani

options.description: Seek further diagnostic tests before deciding on surgery
options.clinical_reasoning: Further diagnostics could clarify the necessity of surgery

actual_action_defendant.description: Surgery was scheduled by Dr. Durrani

expected_action_court.description: Further diagnostics should have been pursued
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert testimony suggested surgery necessity was disputed

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Based on expert testimony disputing surgery necessity
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Further diagnostics should have been conducted

decision_points.clinical_context: Surgery scheduled with Dr. Durrani unavailable

options.description: Proceed with Dr. Shanti performing the surgery
options.clinical_reasoning: Dr. Shanti filled in for Dr. Durrani

options.description: Reschedule surgery for when Dr. Durrani is available
options.clinical_reasoning: Patient consent was for Dr. Durrani

expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Informed consent was for Dr. Durrani

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Informed consent was not obtained for Dr. Shanti

timeline_phases.name: Emergency Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents to ER with knee problem

key_events.event: ER visit due to knee problem

timeline_phases.name: Consultation and Decision for Surgery
timeline_phases.description: Consultation by Dr. Durrani and decision to schedule surgery

patient_outcome.description: Patient developed significant back pain post-surgery

key_findings: Breach of duty in scheduling surgery without proper consent
Causation established - surgery led to new back pain

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to obtain informed consent and unnecessary surgery
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Proper consent and diagnostics could have prevented unnecessary surgery

factual_timeline.event: Patient visits ER for knee problem

court_findings.finding: Dr. Durrani liable for negligence and lack of informed consent
